category_title: Students with moderate to severe cognitive impairments who exhibit withdrawal or 'shut down' behaviors in inclusive settings
scenario_title: Recorder playing and music notation decoding in inclusive setting
minimal_context: Katie is in her inclusive fourth-grade music class attempting to read music notation and play the recorder.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's look at the music notation and play the next section on our recorders.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Holds the recorder but does not cover any of the holes with her fingers, looking at the music notation without decoding it.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Go ahead and play the notes with the rest of the class.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Blows into the instrument without accurate fingerings, hears that her sounds are not the same as those around her, and stops trying to play.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Keep trying to follow the music.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Becomes completely withdrawn and off-task, starts playing with the recorder in her hands, and asks) Can I go to the bathroom? I need a tissue.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's look at the music notation and play the next section on our recorders.

STUDENT: (Holds the recorder but does not cover any of the holes with her fingers, looking at the music notation without decoding it.)

TUTOR: Go ahead and play the notes with the rest of the class.

STUDENT: (Blows into the instrument without accurate fingerings, hears that her sounds are not the same as those around her, and stops trying to play.)

TUTOR: Keep trying to follow the music.

STUDENT: (Becomes completely withdrawn and off-task, starts playing with the recorder in her hands, and asks) Can I go to the bathroom? I need a tissue.

task.content: Recorder-playing activities and attempting to decode music notation

context.social_setting: Sitting next to peers in an inclusive classroom

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When lacking the fine motor coordination to cover holes on a recorder or use the tongue to control articulation, and lacking the cognitive skills required to decode music notation
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Withdrawn and off-task, engaged in behavior such as playing with her recorder, asking for tissues, and trips to the bathroom

behavior_patterns.condition: When attempting to play the recorder despite a lack of prerequisite skills
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Frequently off-task, and even when she 'played,' she was clearly not accurate in her fingerings, or even covering any holes
